description: Fix three bugs in the MCP server: incorrect regex ordering in error sanitization, WebSocket frame sample matching failure when URLs have query params, and detectPrimaryApiBaseUrl using call counts instead of endpoint counts for the shared-prefix threshold.

userStories.title: Fix sanitize-error.ts regex ordering so URLs are fully replaced
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want error messages containing URLs to be fully sanitized to [URL] so that protocol prefixes like 'https:' do not leak to external MCP clients.
acceptanceCriteria: The URL regex (.replace(/https?:\/\/.../) runs BEFORE the Unix path regex (.replace(/\/[a-z].../) in sanitizeErrorMessage
Input 'Failed to connect to https://internal.corp/api/v2' produces 'Failed to connect to [URL]' (not 'Failed to connect to https:[PATH]')
Input 'Error at /usr/local/bin/node' still produces 'Error at [PATH]' (Unix paths without protocol still work)
Input 'Bad path C:\Users\admin\file.txt' still produces 'Bad path [PATH]' (Windows paths still work)
Existing unit tests in sanitize-error.test.ts pass (update any that depended on the old ordering)
userStories.notes:
File: platform/mcp-server/src/sanitize-error.ts (31 lines total).

The bug: line 15 (Unix path regex) runs before line 17 (URL regex). For input like 'https://example.com/api', the Unix path regex matches '/example.com/api' first → 'https:[PATH]'. Then the URL regex fails because '://' is gone.

Fix: Swap line 15 and line 17 — move the URL regex (.replace(/https?:\/\/[^\s,;)}\]]+/gi, '[URL]')) to run before the Unix path regex. The Windows path regex on line 12 must stay first (it uses a different pattern that won't conflict).

New order should be: (1) Windows paths, (2) Full URLs with protocol, (3) Unix paths, (4) localhost, (5) IPv4.

Check platform/mcp-server/src/sanitize-error.test.ts for existing tests. Add a test case for 'https://internal.corp/api/v2' → '[URL]' to prevent regression.

userStories.title: Fix WebSocket frame URL matching in detect-apis.ts
userStories.description: As a plugin developer using browser_analyze_site, I want WebSocket frame samples to be correctly attached to WebSocket endpoints so that I can see real-time API payloads even when the WebSocket URL includes query parameters.
acceptanceCriteria: WebSocket frame URLs are normalized to origin+pathname (stripping query params) before comparison with ep.url
A frame with url 'wss://example.com/ws?token=abc' matches an endpoint with url 'wss://example.com/ws'
Existing unit tests in detect-apis.test.ts pass
A new test verifies frame-to-endpoint matching when the WS URL has query params

userStories.title: Fix detectPrimaryApiBaseUrl to count distinct endpoints
userStories.description: As a plugin developer using browser_analyze_site, I want the primary API base URL detection to identify the deepest path prefix shared by multiple distinct endpoints so that tool suggestions are based on a genuine common base, not an overly-specific single-endpoint path.
acceptanceCriteria: Prefix accumulation counts distinct endpoints contributing to each prefix, not their callCount sums
The minCount threshold is based on endpoint count (endpoints.length), not summed call counts
A single endpoint called 10 times does NOT produce a deep prefix like '/api/v1/users'; it produces at most the origin
Two distinct endpoints sharing '/api/v1' with different leaf paths correctly identify '/api/v1' as primary base
Existing unit tests pass; new test case verifies the fix
userStories.notes:
File: platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/analyze-site/detect-apis.ts, function detectPrimaryApiBaseUrl (around line 330-374).

The bug: At line 342-346, prefixes are accumulated using `endpoint.callCount` instead of 1 per endpoint. At line 355, `totalEndpoints` sums callCounts across all endpoints. This means a single endpoint called N times gets N added to prefix counts, so minCount (line 356) can be satisfied by a single heavily-called endpoint.

Fix: Change the prefix counting to count each endpoint once (use `+1` instead of `+endpoint.callCount`). Change `totalEndpoints` at line 355 to use `endpoints.length` instead of reducing callCounts. This ensures the threshold correctly requires multiple DISTINCT endpoints to share a prefix.

Check platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/analyze-site/detect-apis.test.ts for existing tests.
